page: Google Cloud Platform m4-megamem-56 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the m4 server family. It features 56 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture and is equipped with 744.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 13.29 GB of RAM per core, positioning the instance for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.

The instance does not include integrated local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. This design prioritizes memory density and dedicated CPU allocation over local disk I/O or hardware-accelerated graphics processing. The qualitative cost profile reflects its specialized hardware class, where resource allocation is heavily weighted toward memory capacity rather than general-purpose compute or local storage performance.

Appropriate workloads for the m4-megamem-56 include large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, memory-intensive data analytics, and caching layers. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable processing performance for these memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform m4-megamem-56 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the m4 family, featuring 56 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 744.0 GB of RAM. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 13.29 GB per core, this instance is designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is tailored for workloads such as large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound data processing tasks where massive RAM capacity is required alongside dedicated compute resources.
